mechanism_narrative: A lower policy rate compresses the equity discount rate and supports valuation multiples, so declines in FEDFUNDS should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the move is part of an easing cycle.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than unconditional returns after FEDFUNDS declines, or if the sign relation is negative in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment above a short-run threshold signals deteriorating labor income and weaker demand, which should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next month once the labor market has clearly softened.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the effect disappears in successive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in retail sales indicates stronger household demand and revenue growth expectations, so positive RSAFS momentum should support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not correspond to above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the rank correlation turns negative for any multi-year stretch.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals stronger real activity and better corporate cash-flow prospects, so INDPRO upturns should forecast positive S&P 500 returns over the next month unless the increase is purely inventory rebuilding.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month INDPRO rises are not followed by higher-than-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect is absent when INDPRO is above its prior 6-month trend.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts collapse signals a credit-sensitive demand shock and a coming slowdown in consumption and construction, so sharp HOUST declines should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by at least 10% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST drops are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal reverses after controlling for the prior housing trend.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll growth slows while unemployment rises, income growth and spending expectations deteriorate jointly, so the combination should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next week; neither labor series alone is sufficient.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE deterioration does not outperform either series alone in predicting 5-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal has no directional edge in triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a rising policy rate with still-elevated inflation pressures should compress multiples and raise recession odds, so simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above its prior 6-month average and CPIAUCSL is rising over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined tightening-plus-inflation condition does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term is not materially stronger than the single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, weak GDP growth combined with softer retail sales indicates demand exhaustion and earnings downgrades, so the pair should forecast negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if weak GDPC1 and RSAFS do not jointly precede below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the effect equally well.
